_comment: ShipIt-managed Claude Code settings. Baked into /etc/shipit/managed-settings.json in the session-worker image. The orchestrator always passes --settings to the Claude CLI for the `claude` agent (see src/server/session/claude.ts) so the PreToolUse branch-block hook is always active. The Stop hook self-gates on the SHIPIT_AUTO_CREATE_PR env var, which the orchestrator sets only when autoCreatePr is on — see docs/129-stop-hook-pr-enforcement/plan.md and docs/130-block-branch-ops/plan.md. includeCoAuthoredBy is false so the Claude CLI omits the `Co-Authored-By: Claude` commit trailer and the `🤖 Generated with Claude Code` PR footer — ShipIt owns the commit/PR surface, so the upstream attribution is noise here.
_permissions_comment: SHI-36 / docs/097: make the agent's permission posture EXPLICIT instead of relying on the CLI's headless defaults. `allow` codifies today's effective behavior (the same tools the orchestrator grants via --allowedTools — see AUTO_TOOLS in src/server/session/agents/claude/process.ts) so a future restrictive CLI default change can't silently break editing. `deny` is the load-bearing part: deny always overrides allow, so these paths can never be written even though `Write(**)`/`Edit(**)` are allowed. /etc/shipit/** is the agent's own settings + hooks (it must not rewrite its own permission policy). The rest are the OAuth/credential and CLI-config FILES — listed individually, NOT as a /root/.claude/** or /credentials/** tree glob, on purpose: /root/.claude is a symlink to /credentials/.claude, and the CLI's file-based memory lives at /root/.claude/projects/<cwd>/memory/ inside that same tree. A blanket tree deny would block the agent from updating its own memory (a legitimate Write-tool operation). Denying the specific credential/config files protects the secrets while leaving projects/**/memory/** writable under any path-matcher behavior. Note: these rules govern the file-edit tools (Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit), not arbitrary Bash — full credential isolation + egress control is tracked separately under docs/172-agent-containment.
